# Collaboration Opportunities

This page identifies ways students and collaborators could contribute to the research program.

## For students

Students may be able to contribute through literature reviews, measure searches, study material development, preregistration drafts, survey programming, data cleaning, qualitative coding, R analysis workflows, or manuscript preparation.

Good student projects should have a bounded question, a clear next action, and a realistic timeline.

## For collaborators

Collaborators may be especially helpful for projects involving polarization, social identity, intervention design, open science, AI-assisted research, data science, contemplative science, or cross-cultural psychological research.

## Current contribution areas

- Build reading lists for book-based interventions.
- Compare measures of prosociality, empathy, compassion, and polarization.
- Design identity-based intervention materials.
- Prototype AI-supported research workflows.
- Translate broad ideas into preregistered study designs.

## Contact

For collaboration inquiries, use the contact information on <https://remi-theriault.com/contact/>.
